name: HYROX — Intermediate

workouts.name: Aerobic Base Run
workouts.notes: Start conservatively at true easy pace. Focus on smooth, relaxed running to build your aerobic engine.

workouts.name: Threshold Introduction
workouts.notes: Your first threshold session. Should feel comfortably hard — like a pace you could hold for an hour.

workouts.name: Long Aerobic Run
workouts.notes: Your weekly long run. Stay conversational throughout — this builds your endurance foundation.

weeks.focus: Building aerobic foundation with basic strength integration

workouts.name: Easy Run + Strides
workouts.notes: Add some leg turnover with strides. Focus on smooth acceleration, not all-out sprinting.

workouts.name: VO2max Intervals — 5×800m
workouts.notes: Your first interval session. These should feel hard but sustainable. You're building oxygen processing power.

workouts.name: Progressive Long Run
workouts.notes: Start easy, finish at marathon pace. This teaches your body to run strong when fatigued.

weeks.focus: Introducing interval training with strength awareness

workouts.name: Recovery Run
workouts.notes: Active recovery from yesterday's long run. Keep it truly easy and focus on smooth form.

workouts.name: Extended Threshold
workouts.notes: Longer threshold effort. This pace should feel sustainably hard — like your one-hour race pace.

workouts.name: Steady Long Run
workouts.notes: Your longest run yet. Stay patient and conversational. This builds your aerobic capacity foundation.

workouts.name: Easy Recovery
workouts.notes: Cutback week for recovery. Keep it short and easy to allow adaptation from the previous three weeks.

workouts.name: Short VO2max — 4×600m
workouts.notes: Shorter intervals but same intensity. Focus on smooth, controlled effort rather than fighting the pace.

workouts.name: Reduced Long Run
workouts.notes: Shorter long run for recovery. Use this to practice race-day nutrition and hydration strategies.

weeks.focus: Recovery week — reduced volume, maintained intensity

workouts.name: Easy + Speed Development
workouts.notes: Building back up after recovery week. Strides help maintain speed while building base.

workouts.name: HYROX Simulation Fartlek
workouts.notes: Simulates HYROX running segments. Vary efforts like you'll experience between stations. Practice quick pace changes.

workouts.name: Long Run + Race Pace
workouts.notes: Practice running at target pace when already fatigued. This mimics HYROX running segments after strength stations.

workouts.name: Active Recovery
workouts.notes: Easy shake-out run. Focus on relaxed form and let your body recover from yesterday's efforts.

workouts.name: Lactate Clearance — 4×1200m
workouts.notes: Longer intervals to build lactate processing. These should feel hard but controlled — not all-out.

workouts.name: Negative Split Long Run
workouts.notes: Start conservative, finish strong. This teaches you to run fast on tired legs — crucial for HYROX.

weeks.focus: Developing lactate clearance and strength endurance

workouts.name: Steady State Tempo
workouts.notes: Comfortably hard effort. This pace develops your ability to clear lactate efficiently.

workouts.name: Speed Development — 6×400m
workouts.notes: Fast but controlled. Focus on smooth acceleration and maintaining form at speed.

workouts.name: Steady Endurance Build
workouts.notes: Your longest run yet. Stay patient and conversational. This builds the endurance base for HYROX.

weeks.focus: Mixed pace development and strength integration

workouts.name: Easy Recovery
workouts.notes: Recovery week to absorb the training load. Keep it truly easy and focus on how you feel.

workouts.name: Relaxed Fartlek
workouts.notes: Unstructured speed play. Go by feel — surge when you feel good, ease off when you don't.

workouts.name: Recovery Long Run
workouts.notes: Shorter long run for recovery. Practice your race-day fueling and hydration strategy.

workouts.name: Easy + Neuromuscular Activation
workouts.notes: Building back up with speed elements. Strides activate your fast-twitch fibers for tomorrow's workout.

workouts.name: HYROX Simulation — 8×1000m
workouts.notes: Simulates running between HYROX stations. Practice quick recovery between hard efforts — like after each exercise station.

workouts.name: Long Run + Marathon Pace Blocks
workouts.notes: Practice maintaining target pace when fatigued. This builds the specific endurance needed for HYROX running segments.

weeks.focus: HYROX-specific interval training with strength simulation

workouts.name: Recovery Spin
workouts.notes: Active recovery from yesterday's long effort. Keep it conversational and focus on smooth form.

workouts.name: VO2max Power — 5×800m
workouts.notes: These should feel hard but not all-out. Focus on smooth, powerful running to build your oxygen processing capacity.

workouts.name: Progressive Endurance Run
workouts.notes: Your longest run. Start easy, gradually increase to marathon pace. This builds the ability to run strong when tired.

weeks.focus: Developing anaerobic capacity and recovery speed

workouts.name: Cruise Intervals
workouts.notes: Broken threshold run. These intervals should feel comfortably hard with quick recovery.

workouts.name: Neuromuscular Power — 8×200m
workouts.notes: Short, fast efforts to maintain leg speed. Focus on quick turnover and relaxed form at speed.

workouts.name: Peak Volume Long Run
workouts.notes: Peak distance for this cycle. Stay patient and conversational. This is your biggest aerobic stimulus.

workouts.name: Easy Recovery
workouts.notes: Recovery week to consolidate your fitness gains. Listen to your body and keep it truly easy.

workouts.name: Unstructured Fartlek
workouts.notes: Play with pace based on how you feel. No pressure — just enjoy the variety of speeds.

workouts.name: Recovery Long Run
workouts.notes: Reduced volume for recovery. Use this to practice your race nutrition and pacing strategies.

workouts.name: Easy + Speed Prep
workouts.notes: Entering peak phase. Strides prepare your legs for tomorrow's race-specific work.

workouts.name: HYROX Race Simulation
workouts.notes: Simulate HYROX running segments. Practice quick transitions and maintaining pace after 'strength stations'.

workouts.name: Long Run + HYROX Pace
workouts.notes: Practice your HYROX running pace when already fatigued. This is crucial race-specific preparation.

workouts.name: Active Recovery
workouts.notes: Easy recovery from yesterday's race-pace work. Focus on feeling loose and relaxed.

workouts.name: Lactate Tolerance — 6×600m
workouts.notes: Shorter intervals at high intensity. These build your ability to clear lactate quickly — crucial for HYROX transitions.

workouts.name: Negative Split Long Run
workouts.notes: Practice finishing strong when tired. Start conservative, build to marathon pace for the final third.

workouts.name: Threshold Maintenance
workouts.notes: Maintain your threshold fitness with a solid tempo effort. Should feel controlled and strong.

workouts.name: Speed Sharpening — 6×300m
workouts.notes: Sharpen your leg speed for race day. Focus on smooth, controlled speed rather than all-out effort.

workouts.name: Steady Endurance
workouts.notes: Solid endurance run to maintain your aerobic base. Keep it conversational but purposeful.

workouts.name: Easy Recovery
workouts.notes: Recovery week in the peak phase. Keep it easy and focus on feeling fresh and ready.

workouts.name: Race Prep Fartlek
workouts.notes: Short bursts at various paces to keep your legs sharp. Nothing too hard — just wake up the fast-twitch fibers.

workouts.name: Recovery Long Run
workouts.notes: Shorter long run for recovery. Practice your race-day routine and nutrition strategy.

weeks.focus: Recovery and sharpening before final peak

workouts.name: Easy + Race Prep Strides
workouts.notes: Final preparation phase. Strides keep your legs sharp for the upcoming race simulation.

workouts.name: Final HYROX Simulation
workouts.notes: Your final race simulation. Practice everything: pacing, transitions, fueling. This should feel controlled and confident.

workouts.name: Final Long Run + Race Pace
workouts.notes: Last big effort before taper. Practice your race strategy and build confidence in your fitness.

workouts.name: Taper Easy Run
workouts.notes: Beginning your taper. Focus on feeling fresh and recovered. Your fitness is built — now we maintain it.

workouts.name: Taper Sharpening — 4×800m
workouts.notes: Shorter session to maintain sharpness. These should feel smooth and controlled — not a struggle.

workouts.name: Taper Long Run
workouts.notes: Reduced long run volume. Keep it conversational and focus on feeling strong and relaxed.

weeks.focus: Begin taper — maintain intensity, reduce volume

workouts.name: Easy + Leg Speed
workouts.notes: Short and easy with strides to maintain leg speed. You should feel bouncy and energetic.

workouts.name: Short Tempo
workouts.notes: Brief threshold effort to maintain fitness. Should feel smooth and controlled — not hard.

workouts.name: Reduced Long Run
workouts.notes: Shorter long run to maintain endurance without fatigue. Include some race-pace practice.

weeks.focus: Continue taper — maintain speed, reduce volume further

workouts.name: Short Easy Run
workouts.notes: Very easy run to stay loose. Focus on how good you feel and your race readiness.

workouts.name: Final Sharpener — 4×400m
workouts.notes: Last speed work before race. These should feel fast but effortless. Focus on smooth form and confidence.

workouts.name: Pre-Race Shake Out
workouts.notes: Final longer run before race week. Keep it relaxed and enjoy the feeling of being fit and ready.

workouts.name: Race Week Easy + Strides
workouts.notes: Race week! Keep it short and easy with a few strides to feel sharp. You're ready!

workouts.name: Race Week Shake Out
workouts.notes: Very short, easy run to stay loose. Practice your race-day routine and mindset.

workouts.name: Pre-Race Rest
workouts.notes: Complete rest before race day. Hydrate well, eat familiar foods, and visualize your successful race.

workouts.name: Final Easy Run
workouts.notes: Very short, easy run to stay loose. Focus on feeling relaxed and confident.

workouts.name: Pre-Race Rest
workouts.notes: Complete rest. Focus on hydration, nutrition, and mental preparation for race day.

workouts.name: Race Day Preparation
workouts.notes: HYROX RACE DAY! You've put in the work — now trust your training and execute your race plan. Good luck!

workouts.name: Race Week Rest
workouts.notes: Complete rest to ensure you're fully recovered for race day. Focus on sleep, hydration, and mental preparation.

workouts.name: Pre-Race Rest
workouts.notes: Final rest day. Review your race strategy, prepare your gear, and trust in your excellent preparation.

workouts.name: HYROX RACE DAY
workouts.notes: RACE DAY! Execute your race plan with confidence. You've trained for this moment — now go show what you're capable of!

rationale: This hybrid endurance-strength plan builds from your solid 54.6 VDOT base across three phases. Base phase establishes aerobic foundation, Build phase adds HYROX-specific intervals and functional strength, Peak phase simulates race demands with run-strength combinations, followed by a strategic taper.
tips: HYROX demands both running endurance and functional strength — train both systems consistently
Practice transitions between running and strength stations to minimize time loss on race day
Your 3-day schedule requires quality over quantity — make every session count
Monitor your HRV trends to ensure you're adapting well to the hybrid training load
Simulate race nutrition and hydration strategies during longer combination workouts
athlete_summary: VO2 Max: 54.6 · Resting HR: 40 bpm · Max HR: 230 bpm · Weekly mileage: 15.5 km · Training status: Recovery · LT pace: 4:18/km · LT HR: 160 bpm · Endurance score: 6385.0 · Recent runs: 34 activities, avg 5.9km @ 4:58/km · Pace source: Garmin VO2 Max (54.6) + custom overrides
